cdh_action: Re-run CDH SPEI pipeline globally; FAO ASI complementary
notes: DECISION: adopt SPEI as the climatic drought hazard (hazard slot; crop response handled separately by CCC, so avoid realised-stress ASI here). Deliver by EXTENDING CDH's existing SPEI pipeline to global 0.05deg -- a re-run of a proven pipeline, not a new indicator, within capacity; CHIRPS bounds coverage to 60N-60S (covers essentially all B4T crops); modest compute/storage. SPEIbase (0.5deg global, open) = independent cross-check. FAO ASI (1km, crop-masked, global, open, 1984-present) = best-in-class agri-specific ALTERNATIVE / validation, route to an impact-aware signal later; Copernicus CDI = multi-indicator cousin. SPEI not SPI/WASP -- adds PET, partly overlaps heat hazard (Phase-2). No pure SPI internally (backlog).

cdh_action: Confirm w/ Bert; WRSI if retained; GAEZ / CDH water-balance v2 alt
verification_status: FAO/USGS WRSI + GeoWRSI + FAO GAEZ v4 (2026-07)
notes: CLARIFY: current source mis-cited (Hyman 2025 = fishery paper; likely Glenn Hyman/Alliance; paper not found); unknown if active in production CRI; overlaps LGP rows + drought (double-count risk, Phase-2). IF retained: WRSI = standard operational season-failure index (WRSI<50 = crop failure). L-WRSI = global gridded GeoTIFF dekadal 1982-present open (USGS, landscape-level not crop-specific); crop WRSI (CHIRPS-Croplands) = crop-specific gridded 0.1deg dekadal 2001+ but FEWS regions only (Africa/C.America); global crop-specific = run GeoWRSI on CHIRPS (derivation, aligns with SPEI stack). Alt: FAO GAEZ v4 LGP/reliability (open, ~9km, incl. future); CDH water-balance v2 (NDWS/NDWL) internal. Keep cost-vs-value as B4T economic assumption.

verification_status: CIMA GIRI background paper; WRI Aqueduct Floods Ward 2020; Copernicus CDS sis-water-level-change-indicators-cmip6 (2026-07-03)
notes: GIRI model is RIVERINE only. No open CMIP6 coastal INUNDATION product. Open CMIP6 DRIVER exists: Copernicus CDS sea-level change indicators (Muis 2023, CMIP6 HighResMIP, SSP5-8.5, global, CC-BY, ~2.5km coastal) -- but delivers extreme-sea-level/surge indicators at points, NOT flood extent. Aqueduct Floods coastal confirmed still CMIP5/RCP; Kirezci 2020 = CMIP5/RCP, not openly packaged. Modernized coastal layer = Phase-2 build on the driver, not off-the-shelf.
